fort, Indiana, where banners rela-
tives reside.


When the box was opened at the
undertaking parlors the body was
found to be greatly disfigured.


The funeral of George Bell the
Great Northern fireman, who was
drowned In the engine when the
train plunged into the lake, will
be held at 2 o'clock Sunday from
the parlors of Smith 4 Co., Rev.
Ellery Of Hillyard officiating.


1t is expected that the crane will
probably get hold of the engine
this afternoon and that news of
the finding of the body of Engineer
Munson, the last in the wreck,
will be brought to the city tonight.


Great Northern officials still have
no excuse or explanation to offer
for the disaster beyond a general
denial that anything could possibly
be wrong for which they might be
held responsible.


Coroner McRae of Stevens county
has changed his mind and now
states that he will use his powers
as coroner to investigate the
wreck regardless of the reluctance
of the county prosecutor to attempt
to fix possibility.
